title: Mixed LF packages cause INVALID_ARGUMENT or unexpected model behavior
description: Mixed LF packages uploaded to a participant may cause INVALID_ARGUMENT errors or unexpected model behavior at runtime. Before 2.10.1, there was no startup-time validation to detect incompatible package combinations. The flag unsafe-disable-upgrade-validation=true could bypass remaining guardrails. Daml 2.10.1 adds startup-time package compatibility validation and documents the risks of disabling upgrade validation.
recommendation: Upgrade to Daml 2.10.1 for startup-time package compatibility validation. Do not use unsafe-disable-upgrade-validation=true in production.

oracle.description: All uploaded packages must be validated for mutual LF version compatibility at startup time.
oracle.static_signal: Detection of mixed LF version packages in the same participant
oracle.symbolic_property: forall p1 p2. uploaded(p1) && uploaded(p2) => lf_compatible(p1, p2)

notes: Daml SDK-level bug. Related to CANTON-REL-01. Demonstrates need for package-level compatibility analysis tools. | Marked out-of-scope: runtime-layer bug, not detectable via contract-level static analysis or symbolic execution.
